allies.rationale_text: Cognitive-load-theory helps usability-heuristics reduce working-memory strain by chunking tasks and simplifying interface or process demands.
allies.source_quote: CLT reminds us that working memory is a limited resource.

allies.rationale_text: Problem-framing-and-reframing helps usability-heuristics act as a first cut on the problem instead of a frozen answer to it.

antagonists.rationale_text: First-principles-thinking resists usability-heuristics when the shortcut masks a genuinely novel problem that needs fresh reconstruction from facts.
antagonists.source_quote: First Principles Thinking strips away assumptions and conventional methods.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Usability-heuristics conflicts with first-principles-thinking when applying established usability patterns prevents the novel interface solutions that first-principles design would generate.
structured_tensions.source_quote: usability-heuristics conflicts with first-principles-thinking (first principles thinking) when applying established usability patterns prevents the novel interface solutions that first-principles design would generate.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Usability-heuristics conflicts with optimization-theory when usability satisficing settles for good-enough interaction when optimization would yield measurably superior outcomes.
structured_tensions.source_quote: usability-heuristics conflicts with optimization-theory (optimization theory) when usability satisficing settles for good-enough interaction when optimization would yield measurably superior outcomes.

curation_notes.summary: The file preserves the source's strongest first-order relation surface: load-reducing heuristic support, framing aid, and the tension against overusing familiar shortcuts.
donor_drops: Did not manufacture structured tensions because the raw file is stronger on general antagonism than on explicit named conflict blocks.
open_questions: Critical-thinking is also a source-real antagonist, but first-principles-thinking was the cleaner current-vocabulary anchor.
